---
title: "Steven Le"
image: photo.jpg
toc: false
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 18em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/lesteven24
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/lesteven42/
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:steven812@ucsb.edu
---

Hello!  My name is Steven Le and the website you are currently looking at is a digital portfolio about everything I do and who I am.  If you are interested in my skills, experiences, and interests, please feel free to look through the rest of the website.  

------------------------------------------------------------------------

I am currently a third-year undergraduate student majoring in Environmental Studies (B.S.) and minoring in Earth Science at the University of California, Santa Barbara.  

Through the [FUERTE Fellows](https://fuerte.eemb.ucsb.edu/people/steven-le) program, I was able to become an undergraduate researcher for the [Landscape Ecophysiology And Function (LEAF) Lab](https://anderegglab.eemb.ucsb.edu/), which was where I recently wrapped up presenting a research poster pertaining to leaf angle and water availability.  Additionally, in June 2025, I became a member of the [Bren-Environmental Studies Fellows](https://bren.ucsb.edu/undergrad-mentoring/bren-es-fellows-program/bren-es-fellows/steven-le) program and previously worked as an urban forestry restoration intern for [Your Children's Trees](https://www.yourchildrenstreesatucsb.org/).  

Do no hesitate to contact me through email or LinkedIn!
